channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
item.description: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none of them use today; a majority intend to switch or add providers within the year, many within a quarter. Buying decisions turn on integration and total cost of ownership rather than headline token price — which is fortunate, because most enterprises cannot yet see their unit economics clearly: GPUs sit at half utilizati...

item.title: Microsoft accused of unfair labor practices by US and Canadian video game unions
item.description: Charges filed with the NLRB accuse Xbox of bad faith bargaining.

item.title: Un espace-temps à 5 dimensions est-il la clé des énigmes de la matière noire ?
item.description: La théorie de la matière noire a fait des prédictions couronnées de succès et d'autres moins. Plusieurs théories sont possibles, donnant aux particules de matière noire des caractéristiques différentes, comme leur masse ou leur capacité à entrer tout de même en interaction autrement que par la...

item.title: AI-powered software could recover 300GW of hidden capacity for the US power grid, enough to power thousands of AI data centers
item.description: GridCARE claims its software can unlock 300 GW of hidden transmission capacity, easing rising electricity demand without expanding infrastructure.

item.title: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
item.description: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents still share credentials; and only three in ten isolate their highest-risk agents. The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for agents, spending remains a thin slice of the security budget, and enterprises are evenly split on whethe...

item.title: NTSB investigators confirm Tesla driver overrode Full Self-Driving system in fatal crash
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration is also investigating the crash

item.title: No sleep score, no steps: this Signal smart ring from an ex-Google head is focused on cracking cuffless, calibration-less blood pressure readings
item.description: The Signal Ring has been created by an ex-Google head and "a very smart group of multidisciplined people focused on a single problem" — blood pressure.

item.title: China beats Elon Musk’s Neuralink to the world’s first commercial brain-computer interface implant — car crash victim given coin-sized chip that turns neural signals into hand movements
item.description: Regulatory approval of brain implants in China mean they can now be fixed to the brains of anyone who wants to try them.

item.title: The AMD Ryzen 7 7700X3D chip is now available for $329
item.description: Reasonable specs for a reasonable price.

item.title: Kalshi says it caught Trump’s teleprompter operator insider trading
item.description: Kalshi users betting on what President Donald Trump would say during his speeches were reportedly up against tough competition: the president's teleprompter operator. ABC News reports that federal investigators believe Gabriel Perez - Trump's teleprompter operator since 2016 - used inside information to make bets on Kalshi, a major prediction market platform that allows users […]...

item.title: Everyone Thinks They Have the Diarrhea Parasite
item.description: Cyclosporiasis isn’t the only thing spreading across the US. So is anxiety about getting hit with it.

item.title: Here’s Why Anthropic Is Pushing States to Regulate AI Faster
item.description: The company endorsed landmark AI transparency laws in California and New York last year, but its head of US state and local policy says they may already be outdated.

item.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
item.description: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

item.title: Don't lose sleep over reports of 260 Starlink satellites deorbiting
item.description: SpaceX deorbiting satellites isn't a cause for worry, but scientists are still looking into the environmental impacts of frequent satellite deorbits.

item.title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game creation feature in its mobile app
item.description: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt.

item.title: Russia to launch Rassvet Starlink rival within days with 318-strong satellite constellation expected by 2028
item.description: Russia appears ready to launch more Rassvet satellites, advancing plans for a 318-satellite communications constellation expected by 2028.

item.title: L’Odyssée de Christopher Nolan : où et comment le voir dans la meilleure version possible ?
item.description: Premier long-métrage de fiction entièrement tourné avec des caméras IMAX film, L'Odyssée n'est visible dans sa version la plus fidèle à la vision de Christopher Nolan que dans une poignée de salles à travers le monde. Choisir le bon cinéma et le bon format devient donc un vrai casse-tête. On vous explique où aller, et pourquoi.

item.title: The Odyssey IMAX 70mm screening prep causes confusion as projectors loaded into theaters spark new fan outrage — as some dub Christopher Nolan's filming choice 'anti-art'
item.description: The Odyssey IMAX 70mm screenings can only happen in 40 theaters worldwide — but why do we suddenly think that IMAX is lying about creating new projectors?

item.title: Firefox vs Edge: fresh shots fired in browser wars as Mozilla report accuses Microsoft of using 'harmful design' to undermine choice in Windows 11
item.description: 'Microsoft continues to deploy harmful design to undermine people's browser choice': Mozilla report concludes that Windows still isn't a level playing field.

item.title: Suno trained its AI on millions of songs from YouTube Music, Deezer and other sites, new hack reveals — and critics have branded it 'staggering theft'
item.description: Suno has previously faced criticism for building an AI song creator on copyrighted works, and here's more evidence.

item.title: New York governor says she’s using AI to analyze ‘every single rule’ in the state
item.description: New York Governor Kathy Hochul might have just signed a moratorium on new AI data centers in the state, but she's not against using the technology herself. During an interview with Bloomberg's Odd Lots podcast, Hochul said that her team is using "AI to analyze every single rule, regulation, [and] policy" to check for outdated […]

item.title: Ecovacs’ self-cleaning Deebot X11 has hit a new low price
item.description: Sometimes it feels like keeping your floors clean is one of those never-ending chores, which is why it's nice to have a versatile robot vacuum take it off your hands. The Ecovacs Deebot X11 robovac / mop hybrid is designed to do just that, and it's now $699 ($400 off) at Amazon and directly from […]

item.title: Sonos brings tab navigation, speaker sorting and more to its app
item.description: Sonos has added some handy quality-of-life updates to its mobile app.

item.title: Oil giant BP shutters its corporate venture arm after 20 years
item.description: BP Ventures is shutting down, ending a nearly 20-year run that was marked by reportedly lackluster returns.

item.title: BookTok creators share the reading essentials they actually use
item.description: Mashable spoke with BookTokkers and book lovers at VidCon 2026 about the reading tools and accessories they love.

item.title: ‘Dirty Jobs’ host just revealed the lucrative salary Gen Z electricians make from AI data centers—the number is eye-popping
item.description: There’s a new demand for blue collar jobs, with incomes rising well into the six figures. “Dirty Jobs” founder and TV host Mike Rowe recently said that at a time when many companies are laying off white-collar workers, there is a skilled labor shortage threatening AI’s current $10 trillion infrastructure boom—and solving that gap is key to ensuring the future health of the U.S. economy....

item.title: The Ninja Crispi Microwave automatically air fries reheated food. Your leftover game is about to change.
item.description: Released on July 16, the Ninja Crispi Microwave combines crispy air frying with the speedy reheating and defrosting of a regular microwave.

item.title: 12 actually useful desk upgrades that are all under $100 — I review work gear for a living, and these practical gadgets are more than just office eye-candy
item.description: This is the actually useful desk gear I recommend for the home, office, or dorm room.
